texto: Nothing new about death, nothing new about deaths caused militarily. We scorched and boiled and baked to death more people in Tokyo on that night of March 9-10 than went up in vapor at Hiroshima and Nagasaki combined.

frase_original: Nothing new about death, nothing new about deaths caused militarily. We scorched and boiled and baked to death more people in Tokyo on that night of March 9-10 than went up in vapor at Hiroshima and Nagasaki combined.

texto: Killing Japanese didn’t bother me at that time. It was getting the war over with that bothered me. So I wasn’t worried particularly about how many people we killed in getting the job done… . All war is immoral, and if you let it bother you, you’re not a good soldier.
explicacao: Citação de Ronald Schaffer sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “Killing Japanese didn’t bother me at that time. It was getting the war over with that bothered me…” articula reflexao, conscience, bother em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.
